name: jayanti (daughter)
shortDescription: Jayanti is the daughter of Indra, the king of gods, and the wife of Shukra, the preceptor of the Asuras. She is known for her devotion and the role she played in Shukra's attainment of the Mrita Sanjeevani mantra.
description: Jayanti is a significant, though not widely prominent, figure in Hindu mythology. She is primarily known as the daughter of Indra, the powerful king of the gods (Devas), and the wife of Shukra, the guru of the Asuras (demons). Her story is primarily found within narratives concerning Shukra's acquisition of the Mrita Sanjeevani mantra, a powerful incantation capable of restoring life. According to the Devi Bhagavata Purana, Shukra performed severe penance to Lord Shiva to obtain this mantra. During this period of intense austerity, Indra, fearing Shukra's growing power, attempted to disrupt his meditation. Jayanti, demonstrating her piety and devotion to her husband, protected Shukra from Indra's interference. She served him faithfully throughout his penance, shielding him from harm and ensuring the success of his endeavor. Impressed by her devotion and Shukra's perseverance, Shiva granted Shukra the Mrita Sanjeevani mantra. Jayanti's role is crucial in this narrative, as her unwavering support and protection were instrumental in Shukra's success. Her story highlights the importance of devotion, loyalty, and the power of a wife's dedication in Hindu mythology. While she doesn't have a vast independent mythology, her connection to Indra and Shukra places her within the central pantheon and highlights the complex relationships between the Devas and Asuras.
